title: Grammys head-scratcher: How did Billie Eilisch win 2026's Song of the Year for her 2024 hit 'Wildflower'?
text:
Billie Eilisch's "Wildflower" Song of the Year Win: Eligibility Analysis

How It Became Eligible: Despite appearing on Eilsch's 2024 album Hit Me Hard and Soft, "Wildflower" wasn't released as a single until February 2025—after the previous year's Grammy eligibility window had closed. The Recording Academy distinguishes between tracks (songs released as part of albums) and singles (songs released separately), treating them as separate entities for award purposes.

Since the 68th Grammy Awards covered submissions from August 31, 2024 through August 30, 2025, the song qualified for consideration in this cycle.

Eligibility Rules Applied: According to the Academy's rulebook, recordings from prior-year albums can compete subsequently "provided the recording was not entered as a track or single the previous year." Critically, Eilsch's album Hit Me Hard and Soft didn't win in any performance category at the 67th Grammys, removing a potential disqualification barrier.

The Surprise Victory: Ranked sixth in pre-show odds, Eilsch defeated favored contenders like "Golden" by KPop Demon Hunters (56% odds) and Lady Gaga's "Abracadabra" (13.1% odds). This marked her third Song of the Year win with collaborator Finneas—a record achievement in that category.
